concept: Beauty / Aesthetic Quality / That Which Delights
description: The quality that gives pleasure to the senses or mind; what evokes appreciation through form. 'Mioru' names what stops us with wonder—the quality that makes us see.
sound_symbolism: The word beholds with wonder. 'Mi' meets with appreciating gaze, beauty encountered. 'O' opens with wonder expanding, awe arising. 'Ru' runs with aesthetic flow, beauty moving through.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo mioru' (the beauty). What 'shela' (art) expresses. Related to 'wela' (good/beautiful) as noun to quality.

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Beauty as essential—not decoration but fundamental to meaningful life.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Beauty as impermanent—the cherry blossom's brief bloom, more precious for its passing.
